message:
fix(security,supply_chain): length-prefixed hashing, domain-mixed digests, and NaN-safe canonicalization

Harden cryptographic digests across trust identity, artifact signing,
proof execution, and metrics subsystems:

- trust_object_id: derive_content_addressed() and derive_epoch_sequenced()
  now use length-prefixed, domain-mixed hashing so cross-domain ID
  collisions are impossible (Extension:sha256:X != TrustCard:sha256:X)
- artifact_signing: KeyTransitionRecord::canonical_bytes() gains a domain
  separator and length-prefixed variable-length fields to prevent
  concatenation ambiguity
- proof_executor: correct domain tag from proof_executor_digest_v1 to
  proof_executor_signature_v1
- adversarial/performance metrics: hash NaN/Inf f64 as 0u64 for
  deterministic digests (NaN has multiple bit representations)
- category_shift: reject non-finite claim values, use safe slice indexing
- migration_kit: validate min_api_coverage_pct is finite before comparison

Update epoch_scoped_keys test vectors to match new hash outputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
